blurb: Combat Swords is the standard raiding rogue of TBC: Sinister Strike, five-point Slice and Dice forever, Adrenaline Rush + Blade Flurry on cooldown. It is the most gear-forgiving melee spec in the game, and the phase lists below are the reason 'rogue' and 'swords' were synonyms for two years. This Phase 2 snapshot aggregates 281 top DPS parses from Serpentshrine Cavern and Tempest Keep; the highest-consensus pick is Deathmantle Handguards at 97% usage.
statPriorityRationale: Combat's white-damage engine makes weapon-skill consistency king — hit and expertise before everything, because Sinister Strike dodges bleed rage-equivalent energy and break Slice uptime.

faq.question: Where does Combat Rogue Phase 2 BiS come from?
faq.answer: From the gear worn by 281 top-ranked Combat Rogue DPS parses on Warcraft Logs during Phase 2 (Serpentshrine Cavern and Tempest Keep), aggregated per slot — what high parsers actually equip, not a spreadsheet ideal.

faq.question: What are the highest-consensus Combat Rogue items in Phase 2?
faq.answer: Deathmantle Handguards (97%), Edgewalker Longboots (96%), Drape of the Dark Reavers (93%). When an item sits above ~70% among top parsers, treat it as the priority drop to chase.

faq.question: Should I follow this list exactly for Phase 2?
faq.answer: Use it as a loot-priority guide, not a checklist — expand each slot's alternatives, because second-choice items are often within a few percent and drop from entirely different bosses in Serpentshrine Cavern and Tempest Keep.
